Forget the shiny modern forwards, who was the real Premier League scoring king of the early 2000s?

Thierry Henry? 4x PL Golden Boots, 175 goals, the Invincible. Pure flair, pace, and power. Could do it all.

Ruud van Nistelrooy? 95 PL goals in just 150 games. Unmatched penalty box instinct. Ruthless.

One was pure artistry, the other pure killer instinct. Who had the bigger impact?

Milan's European pedigree is UNDENIABLE.

They're the ONLY Italian club to have lifted the Champions League/European Cup SEVEN times.

Think about that. Seven! While others chased domestic glory, Milan were conquering Europe. Absolute giants of the game. Let that sink in.

The Ballon d'Or roll call is elite, but one name stands alone.

George Weah in 1995 wasn't just winning football's top prize; he was shattering glass ceilings for a continent. Power, pace, and pure brilliance. A truly unique talent whose impact goes far beyond the pitch. Legend status secured forever.

His solo goals for Milan, the sheer POWER, the skill... he was unstoppable before "solo goal" was even a trend. A true legend who defied expectations! #Weah #BallonDor #ACMilan #AfricanLegends
